message:
Fix axis-aware ReduceMax/ReduceMin VJP, prevent TTL cache memory leak, and materialize dangling e-graph literals

- fj-ad/lib.rs: Rewrite ReduceMax/ReduceMin backward pass to be axis-aware.
  The old approach used flat scalar comparison which broke for partial reductions.
  The new approach broadcasts output and gradient to input shape, builds an
  indicator mask via Eq, counts ties per reduction window via ReduceSum, and
  distributes the gradient share proportionally. Also add an explicit error for
  partial reduce_prod VJP (axes parameter) instead of silently producing wrong
  gradients.

- fj-cache/eviction.rs: Fix memory leak in TtlLruCache where insert_times could
  grow unboundedly after TTL sweeps removed entries from the inner LRU but not
  from insert_times. When insert_times exceeds 2x max_entries, it now locks the
  inner LRU order deque, builds a HashSet of active keys, and retains only
  matching entries. The order field visibility is widened to pub(crate) for this.

- fj-egraph/lib.rs: Fix dangling literal outputs in egraph_to_jaxpr. When the
  last e-graph node is a literal (Num or Symbol starting with f64:/bool:), a
  synthetic Primitive::Select equation is emitted so the literal is materialized
  as a proper JAXPR output variable. Also use saturating_sub(1) for safety in
  output variable lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
